AND 4) REQUESTS FOR ADMIISSIONS, SET ONE, TO DEFT TODD DAUM FILED BY PLAINTIFF GENERAL WORKING INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION GENERAL WORKINGS INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION HEARING SET FOR SEP-25-2013 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT TODD DAUM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY REGARDING DEFENDANT DAUM'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD MORE FACTS REGARDING DEFENDANT DAUM'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SERVICES RENDERED IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT IT PERFORMED IT SERVICES FOR THE DIRECT BENEFIT OF DEFENDANT, AND THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PAY THE REASONABLE VALUE OF SERVICES, $75,000. (FAC 33-37.) DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT DEFENDANT KNEW OR HAD REASON TO KNOW THAT THE ALLEGED TRADE SECRETS WERE ACQUIRED BY IMPROPER MEANS. (FAC 46.) WHETHER THE COMMON LAW CLAIMS ARE PREEMPTED BY CIV. CODE 3426 ET SEQ IS A FACTUAL QUESTION. (SEE KC MULITIMEDIA, INC. V. BANK OF AMERICA TECHNOLOGY & OPERATIONS, INC. (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 939, 954.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TODD DAUM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action, and overruled as to the fourth and sixth causes of action. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's demurrer to the second and third causes of action is sustained. Plaintiff has failed to plead facts with sufficient specificity regarding Defendant Daum's alleged misrepresentation. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to plead more facts regarding Defendant Daum's alleged misrepresentation. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for services rendered is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that it performed IT services for the direct benefit of Defendant, and that Defendant did not pay the reasonable value of services, $75,000. (FAC 33-37.) Defendant's demurrer to the sixth cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that Defendant knew or had reason to know that the alleged trade secrets were acquired by improper means. (FAC 46.) Whether the common law claims are preempted by Civ. Code 3426 et seq is a factual question. (See KC Mulitimedia, Inc. v. Bank of America Technology & Operations, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 939, 954.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
